steps.title: Set up the per-acre inequality
steps.content: The biologist estimates at least 40 and no more than 55 spruce trees per acre: $40 \leq \text{trees per acre} \leq 55$.

steps.title: Scale to 4 acres
steps.content: Multiply every part of the inequality by 4: $40 \times 4 = 160$ and $55 \times 4 = 220$, giving $160 \leq x \leq 220$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $40 \leq x \leq 55$: These are the per-acre bounds, not scaled to 4 acres. Eliminated.
  • Choice B — $160 \leq x \leq 220$: Lower bound $40 \times 4 = 160$, upper bound $55 \times 4 = 220$. That confirms B is correct.
  • Choice C — $40 \leq x \leq 220$: Lower bound is unscaled while upper bound is scaled; inconsistent. Eliminated.
  • Choice D — $160 \leq x \leq 55$: Lower bound is greater than upper bound, which is impossible. Eliminated.
